parsed_review.summary: CP00-891 (RP29 Commercial Readiness P08 Hermes evidence contract-tail + type/shape head, risk A, 10 units RP29.P08.M01.S06-RP29.P08.M02.S07) is a sound descriptor-only pack with no overclaiming. The CP891 binding (registry.js:371-386) matches the live closeout-pack-plan, manifest baseline, and alias/canonical contracts; section-row slices map exactly to the 3 Contract-Draft + 7 Type/Shape unit titles. Hermes evidence rows (registry.js:1978-2013) carry every no-runtime/no-write/no-secret/no-real-data flag plus human_final_approval_required:true, and validators.js:2430-2491 enforces these boundaries, the authority boundary, Hermes/Claude packet shapes, and coverage distribution. The generated commercial-readiness-contract.json rows match the descriptor exactly with validation.valid:true; model.test.js asserts empty error arrays against the live plan. Boundaries hold: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, read_only=true, emits_runtime_receipt=false; enterprise trust and runtime opening are deferred to CP892 under human approval, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt and descriptor evidence, not product runtime. The manifest correctly reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status in_progress; adjudication and construction-inspection accurately reflect the pending-review state and defer promotion to this review plus the deterministic validation ladder. Review-evidence hardening checks pass (auth preflight exit 0 with 1456-byte stdout; no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output), and unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook, ldip) are correctly segregated out of pack scope. The full deterministic validation ladder (npm test, npm run validate, etc.) must still run post-review per command-evidence notes, but that is the normal pipeline and not a review block. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
